blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
Here are the best Android apps available on the Google Play Store for playing **Blind Man's Bluff** (also widely known as **Indian Poker**):

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game** (by Vertex AI Search)
This is a dedicated, highly polished single-player card game built entirely around the rules of Blind Man's Bluff. 
* **How it works:** You are dealt a card face-down that you cannot see, but your opponent's card is shown face-up in the center. You must read the AI's betting patterns and facial expressions to guess the value of your own card.
* **Key Features:** 
  * Three AI difficulty levels (Easy, Normal, and Hard) with varying bluffing behaviors.
  * An "expression mind game" mechanic where you can change your avatar's expression (smirk, sweat, anger) to manipulate the AI's decisions.
  * 100% offline play with no Wi-Fi or account required.

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
A multiplayer-focused adaptation of the classic forehead card game designed for playing with others.
* **How it works:** It brings the traditional "card-on-forehead" experience to your mobile screen. You must predict whether you will win based solely on the card your opponent is holding.
* **Key Features:**
  * Designed to be played locally with friends and family.
  * Focuses heavily on reading your opponent's real-life expressions and psychological bluffing.
  * Clean, simple user interface optimized for quick rounds.

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
A lightweight, no-nonsense simulation app designed to facilitate the game in a group setting.
* **How it works:** It acts as a digital deck helper for playing the game in person. Instead of carrying physical cards, players can use their Android devices to display their cards to everyone else while keeping their own hidden.
* **Key Features:**
  * Extremely lightweight and fast to set up.
  * Intended for local multiplayer groups.

forehead poker app Android.response:
If you are looking to play **Forehead Poker** (also widely known as **Blind Man's Bluff** or **Indian Poker**—where you can see everyone's cards except your own), there are a few great dedicated options available on the Google Play Store for Android:

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game**
* **Best for:** Single-player offline gameplay against AI.
* **Description:** **Blindex** is a psychological card game built entirely around the Blind Man's Bluff / Forehead Poker mechanic. You are dealt a card face-down that you cannot see, but your AI opponent can. Meanwhile, you can see the AI's card. You must read the AI's facial expressions, emotes, and betting patterns to guess the strength of your own card. 
* **Key Features:** It features three AI difficulty levels (Easy, Normal, and Hard), an offline mode (no Wi-Fi required), and a customization shop to unlock different avatars, card decks, and table themes using in-game chips.

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Best for:** Local multiplayer with friends and family.
* **Description:** This app digitizes the classic party game. It is designed to be played with others, allowing you to look at your opponent's card on-screen to predict your victory. 
* **Key Features:** It focuses heavily on the psychological aspect of the game, encouraging you to read your friends' real-life expressions and bluffing habits while playing.

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Best for:** A simple, no-frills digital deck setup.
* **Description:** If you want a straightforward simulation of the classic card-on-forehead game without complex graphics, this app acts as a digital card dealer for a group. 
* **Key Features:** It is intended for multiple people sitting together, replacing the need for a physical deck of cards to play the game. 

*(Note: If you are looking for **Teen Patti**, which is sometimes colloquially referred to as "Indian Poker" but is actually a 3-card brag/poker variant where you do look at your own cards, popular options on the Play Store include **Teen Patti Gold** or **Teen Patti Octro**).*

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
If you are looking for an Android app where you cannot see your own cards but can see everyone else's, you are likely looking for one of two popular card game concepts: **Blind Man's Bluff (Indian Poker)** or the cooperative game **Hanabi**. 

The best Android apps available on the Google Play Store for both of these styles include:

### 1. Blind Man's Bluff / Indian Poker Apps
In these games, players hold their cards facing outward (traditionally on their foreheads). You must bet or make moves based entirely on reading your opponents' cards and their reactions, without ever seeing your own hand.

*   **Blindex - Blind Card Game**  
    This is a highly polished, single-player offline card game built entirely around the Blind Man's Bluff mechanic. You play head-to-head against an AI dealer. Your card faces out (visible to the AI), and the AI's card is visible to you, but neither of you can see your own card. You have to read the AI's "expressions" (like sweating or smirking) and betting patterns to guess if your card is higher or lower.
*   **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)  
    If you want to play this style of game with friends and family, this app is designed for local multiplayer. It allows you to pass the phone or play together, using psychology and your opponents' expressions to predict your victory based only on the cards you can see.

---

### 2. Hanabi Apps
*Hanabi* is a famous cooperative card game where players are trying to launch a perfect fireworks show. The twist is that you hold your cards facing away from you—you can see everyone else's cards, but you have no idea what is in your own hand and must rely on limited clues from your teammates.

*   **Boardible**  
    Boardible is a digital board game platform available on the Google Play Store that features an officially licensed, highly-rated digital version of **Hanabi**. It is one of the best ways to play the game on a mobile device with friends online or locally.
*   **Board Game Arena**  
    While not a standalone app on the Play Store, *Board Game Arena* is a fully mobile-optimized web platform that runs perfectly in any Android browser. It hosts the most popular and active online version of **Hanabi**, allowing you to play in real-time or turn-based modes with players worldwide.
*   **Hanabi Tracker**  
    If you already own the physical *Hanabi* card game, this companion app is incredibly useful. Because you aren't allowed to look at your own cards, this app acts as a utility helper to keep track of the clues your teammates have given you (e.g., "this card is red," "these two cards are 5s") so you don't have to memorize them.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The type of game you are thinking of is a modern, digital version of charades or "headbands." On Android, instead of holding a physical card, you hold your smartphone to your forehead with the screen facing your friends. They act out or shout clues, and you try to guess the word on the screen. 

The most popular and highly-rated apps for this on the Google Play Store include:

### 1. **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)
This is the official, incredibly popular game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres. 
* **How it works:** You select a category (such as Blockbuster Movies, Animals, Accents, or Celebrities), hold the phone to your forehead, and try to guess the words before the 60-second timer runs out. 
* **Controls:** Tilt the phone **down** when you guess correctly to score a point, or tilt it **up** to skip the word. 
* **Bonus:** It can use your phone’s front-facing camera to record video of your friends frantically acting out the clues so you can watch and share the hilarity afterward.

### 2. **Charades & Headbands: Guess Up** (by Cosmicode)
A highly-rated and feature-rich alternative to the original *Heads Up!*
* **How it works:** It features over 100 diverse decks, including pop culture categories like Netflix, Harry Potter, Superheroes, and classic party categories like "Act It Out". 
* **Key Features:** It includes a "Team Mode" for larger groups, allows you to create your own custom card decks, and also supports video recording of your friends' reactions.

### 3. **Heads Up! Netflix Edition** (by Netflix, Inc.)
If you have an active Netflix subscription, you can play this version completely free of ads and in-app purchases.
* **How it works:** It features the exact same "hold to your forehead" gameplay mechanics but is packed with exclusive decks themed around popular Netflix shows and movies (like *Stranger Things*, *Squid Game*, *Bridgerton*, and more).

### 4. **ForeHeads: Charades Guess Game**
Another excellent, family-friendly option designed specifically for group gatherings.
* **How it works:** It offers dozens of unique decks and thousands of word challenges. It is highly praised for having a clean, intuitive interface and offering a great selection of free decks to get the party started.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
Here are some of the best real, existing single-player, offline bluffing card games (and closely related classic bluffing games) available on the Google Play Store:

### 1. **Cheat! – Bluffing Card Game**
* **What it is:** A dedicated single-player adaptation of the classic card game "Cheat" (also known as Bluff, BS, or I Doubt It).
* **How it plays:** You play against three CPU opponents. The objective is to get rid of all your cards by playing them face-down and declaring their rank. You can tell the truth or bluff, but if an AI calls your bluff and catches you, you take a penalty. 
* **Why it fits:** It is fully offline, designed specifically for single-player sessions against AI, and features strategic items (like Lie Counters and Bluff Shields) to spice up the classic rules.

### 2. **Bluff Card Game** (by Arclite Games / Vertex)
* **What it is:** Another highly-rated offline version of the classic "I Doubt It" / "Cheat" card game.
* **How it plays:** It offers a 2-Player Mode (for quick, strategic 1v1 matches against a smart AI) and a 4-Player Mode (for a full table of unpredictable AI opponents). 
* **Why it fits:** It is built from the ground up for offline play (no Wi-Fi required) and features dynamic AI difficulty that adapts to your bluffing patterns.

### 3. **Coup Board Game**
* **What it is:** The digital adaptation of the wildly popular tabletop bluffing card game *Coup*.
* **How it plays:** Each player holds two secret character cards (like the Assassin, Duke, or Captain). On your turn, you can claim to have any character to use their powerful abilities—even if you are lying. Other players can challenge your claim, leading to tense standoffs.
* **Why it fits:** While it has online multiplayer, the app features a robust offline mode where you can play single-player matches against AI bots to hone your deception skills.

### 4. **Liar's Dice Online Multiplayer** (by YoAmbulante)
* **What it is:** While technically a dice-based bluffing game rather than a card game, *Liar's Dice* (also known as Perudo or Pirate Dice) is the ultimate game of deception.
* **How it plays:** Players roll dice in secret and take turns bidding on how many dice of a certain face value are on the table. You must bluff about your own roll to trick opponents into overbidding, then call their bluff when they go too far.
* **Why it fits:** Despite "Online" being in the title, the app features a dedicated **Offline AI Practice** mode. It is one of the most polished bluffing games on Android and is fully playable single-player against smart bots without an internet connection.

best offline single player card game And.response:
Here are the best offline, single-player card games available on the Google Play Store for Android:

### 1. **Balatro**
* **Developer:** Playstack
* **Description:** A massive hit in the card game genre, *Balatro* is a hypnotically satisfying poker-themed roguelike deckbuilder. Your goal is to play illegal poker hands to score chips and defeat "Blinds" (bosses). Along the way, you buy and equip game-changing Joker cards, Tarot cards, and Planet cards that trigger massive multipliers and break the rules of traditional poker. It is fully playable offline and offers endless replayability.

### 2. **Slay the Spire**
* **Developer:** Humble Games
* **Description:** Widely considered the gold standard of roguelike deckbuilders. You choose one of four distinct heroes, each with their own unique card pool and playstyle, to climb a procedurally generated spire. As you defeat enemies, you draft new cards and discover powerful relics that synergize to create incredibly strong combinations. It is a premium game with zero microtransactions and works flawlessly offline.

### 3. **Dawncaster: Deckbuilding RPG**
* **Developer:** Wanderlost Interactive
* **Description:** Designed specifically for mobile, *Dawncaster* is a premium, portrait-mode deckbuilder that blends card mechanics with dark fantasy RPG elements. It features over 900 handcrafted cards, multiple character classes (like Rogue, Warrior, and Seeker), and a unique energy system. It is entirely single-player, offline-friendly, and has no ads or pay-to-win microtransactions.

### 4. **Night of the Full Moon**
* **Developer:** Giant Network
* **Description:** A beautifully illustrated, dark fairy-tale-themed roguelike card game based on the story of Little Red Riding Hood. You explore a mysterious forest, choosing your path and battling over 90 different enemies in turn-based card combat. It features multiple classes (like Nun, Ranger, and Knight), a light auto-chess mode, and a highly engaging story that changes based on your choices. It is fully playable offline.

### 5. **Meteorfall: Journeys**
* **Developer:** Slothwerks
* **Description:** If you want a card game that is easy to play with one hand on the go, *Meteorfall* is the perfect choice. It uses a simple "swipe left or right" mechanic (similar to Tinder) to decide whether to play a card or skip a turn to regain stamina. Despite its simple controls, it offers deep tactical deck-building and a charming, cartoonish art style. 

### 6. **Card Thief**
* **Developer:** Tinytouchtales
* **Description:** A unique blend of solitaire and stealth puzzle gaming. You play as a thief represented by a card on a 3x3 grid. Your goal is to sneak past guards, extinguish torches, pickpocket treasures, and escape without getting caught. It requires careful planning and pathfinding, making it a highly tactical and rewarding offline puzzle card game.

blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
For Android, the best app for **Blind Man's Bluff** (also known as **Indian Poker** or **Forehead Poker**) is **Blindex**.

**Blindex** is a free, offline single-player card game where you play head-to-head against an AI dealer, trying to guess the value of your own card by reading the AI's expressions and betting patterns [1][3].

| Feature | Details |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Gameplay** | Single-player vs. AI with three difficulty levels (each with a unique bluffing style and "tell" to read) [1] |
| **Offline** | 100% offline; no internet connection required [1][3] |
| **Cost** | 100% free; no ads, no in-app purchases [1] |
| **Availability** | Available worldwide on Google Play for Android [1] |
| **Languages** | English & Korean (auto-detects device language) [3] |
| **Extras** | Cosmetic shop for hairstyles, outfits, card decks, and tables [1] |

You can download **Blindex** directly from the Google Play Store: [Blindex on Google Play](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.blindex.app) [3].

*Note: There is another app called "Bluff" (available via APK on Uptodown), but it is a different strategic multiplayer game where players lie about card ranks to get rid of cards, not the "Blind Man's Bluff" Indian Poker mechanic [2].*

forehead poker app Android.response:
There is no app called “forehead poker” for Android, but you likely mean **Indian Poker** (the card-on-forehead game) or a **face-to-face poker app** where cards are shown on your face via video. For Android, the best real poker apps are:

- **Poker Face: Texas Holdem Poker** – A free Texas Hold’em game with video chat to play face-to-face with friends online [2].  
- **Face Poker – Live Video Poker** – A live video poker app that lets you play face-to-face with friends from home [1].  
- **EasyPoker** – A free face-to-face poker app designed for playing with friends in the same location, acting as a digital poker set [4][8].  
- **Poker Heat™ Texas Holdem Poker** – A Vegas-style casino poker app with live games and epic leagues [5].

If you specifically want to play **Indian Poker** (card-on-forehead), there is no dedicated Android app for it yet, but you can play it manually using physical cards or use **EasyPoker** to track chips and hands while playing the forehead version with friends [4][8].

For real-money mobile poker, **CoinPoker** is the top pick in 2026 and works instantly via browser on Android without download [3].

All recommended apps are available on **Google Play Store** for Android.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
The card game you’re describing is **Cabo** (also spelled **Cabo**), where players hold face-down cards they can’t see except for a limited initial peek, and the goal is to end with the lowest score.

For Android, there isn’t a dedicated official app for Cabo yet, but you can play it using:

- **Card Game Simulator** ([2]) – A virtual tabletop app that lets you create, import, and play custom card games with friends. You can manually set up a Cabo game using its custom card import feature and play it online or locally.

If you’re looking for similar memory-based or face-down card games on Android, consider:

- **Card Crawl Adventure** ([5]) – A roguelike deck-building card game with strategic face-down elements (though not exactly Cabo).
- **Jaipur** ([6]) – A trading card game with hidden information mechanics, available as an Android app.
- **Star Realms** or **Hero Realms** ([6]) – Deck-building games with hidden card strategies, both available on Android.

Since Cabo is a physical tabletop game, the best mobile option is to use **Card Game Simulator** to recreate and play it digitally with friends.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The game you’re describing is **Heads Up!** (also known as **Heads Up**, **Heads Up!**, or **Charades & Headbands**), where one player holds a phone or card to their forehead with the screen/card facing outward, and friends give clues to help them guess the word.

For Android, the official app is:

- **Heads Up!** by Warner Bros. ([Google Play link](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.wb.headsup)) – The most popular version with 60+ categories like celebrities, animals, accents, and more. Players hold the phone to their forehead, guess words from friends’ clues, and tilt the phone down (correct) or up (pass).

Other good Android alternatives:

- **Charades & Headbands Game** ([Google Play link](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=net.artgamestudio.charadesapp)) – Similar concept with 60+ charades decks; you place the device on your forehead and guess from friends’ dances, mimes, or clues.

The physical card game version is called **Hedbanz**, where you clip a picture card to a headband on your forehead and guess via yes/no questions ([YouTube demo](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TeyLMzdZN3A)). But for Android, **Heads Up!** is the direct match.
